question_text: What is a Docker container?
correct_answer: A Docker container is a lightweight
explanation: standalone, executable package that includes everything needed to run an application.

question_text: What is the docker-compose.yml file?
correct_answer: docker-compose.yml is a YAML file that defines services
explanation: networks, and volumes for Docker Compose.

question_text: What is the difference between COPY and ADD in Dockerfile?
explanation: while ADD can also handle URLs and extract tar files.

question_text: What is the difference between CMD and ENTRYPOINT?
correct_answer: CMD sets default commands that can be overridden
explanation: ENTRYPOINT sets main command that always runs.

question_text: What is Docker's official image repository?
option_d: Docker Hub's Official Images are curated
explanation: and maintained by Docker.

question_text: What are Docker container restart policies?
explanation: on-failure, no (default).

question_text: What is Docker's layered filesystem?
correct_answer: Docker uses a layered filesystem where each instruction creates a new layer
explanation: enabling efficient storage.

question_text: What command shows all running containers?
explanation: docker ps lists running containers (docker ps -a shows all).

question_text: Which flag runs a container in detached (background) mode?
explanation: docker run -d starts container in background.

question_text: What is the purpose of docker stop vs docker kill?
explanation: docker stop sends SIGTERM ? gives time to shutdown gracefully; kill sends SIGKILL.

question_text: Which command removes a stopped container?
explanation: docker rm <container> removes stopped containers.

question_text: What does docker start do to an existing container?
explanation: docker start <name> starts a previously created/stopped container.

question_text: Which command shows logs of a running container?
explanation: docker logs <container> shows stdout/stderr.

question_text: What flag attaches your terminal to container logs?
explanation: docker logs -f follows live logs (tail -f style).

question_text: Which command executes a command inside a running container?
explanation: docker exec -it <container> bash opens interactive shell.

question_text: What does -it flag mean in docker run / exec?
explanation: -it = --interactive --tty (keeps STDIN open + allocates pseudo-TTY).

question_text: Which command lists all Docker images on your machine?
explanation: All three commands (docker images

question_text: What command pulls an image from Docker Hub?
explanation: docker pull nginx:latest downloads the image.

question_text: Which command tags an existing image?
explanation: docker tag ubuntu:22.04 myubuntu:latest creates new tag.

question_text: What command removes unused Docker images?
explanation: docker image prune removes dangling images (no tags).

question_text: Which flag forces removal of an image even if used by containers?
explanation: docker rmi -f <image> forces removal.

question_text: What is the main purpose of a Dockerfile?
option_a: Define how to build an image
explanation: Dockerfile contains instructions to build a Docker image.

question_text: Which instruction sets the base image in Dockerfile?
explanation: FROM ubuntu:22.04 is always the first non-comment instruction.

question_text: What does RUN instruction do in Dockerfile?
explanation: RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y nginx runs during image build.

question_text: Which instruction copies files from host to image?
explanation: ADD can copy + untar + fetch URL; COPY is preferred for local files.

question_text: What is the purpose of WORKDIR in Dockerfile?
explanation: WORKDIR /app changes current directory for subsequent instructions.

question_text: Which instruction exposes container ports?
explanation: EXPOSE 80 informs which ports container listens on (not publishing).

question_text: What does VOLUME instruction do?
explanation: VOLUME /data declares mount point (anonymous volume if not mounted).

question_text: Which instruction sets environment variables?
explanation: ENV APP_ENV=production sets env vars available at build & runtime.

question_text: What is the recommended way to run multi-stage builds?
explanation: Multi-stage: multiple FROM statements (copy artifacts from one stage to another).

question_text: Which command builds an image from Dockerfile?
explanation: docker build -t myapp:1.0 .

question_text: What flag gives a name/tag to the built image?
explanation: docker build -t myapp:latest .

question_text: Which file is ignored by default during docker build?
explanation: .dockerignore excludes files from build context (like .git

question_text: What command pushes an image to registry?
explanation: docker push myrepo/app:1.0 pushes tagged image.

question_text: Which command logs you into Docker Hub?
explanation: docker login asks for username/password or token.

question_text: What is the default registry when no registry is specified?
explanation: Docker assumes hub.docker.com if no registry prefix.

question_text: Which file defines multi-container applications?
explanation: docker-compose.yml is the standard name (v2/v3).

question_text: What command starts services defined in compose file?
explanation: docker-compose up starts and attaches to services.

question_text: Which flag runs compose in detached mode?
explanation: docker-compose up -d runs in background.

question_text: What command stops and removes containers created by compose?
explanation: docker-compose down stops & removes containers

question_text: Which section in compose file defines persistent storage?
explanation: volumes: section can define named volumes or bind mounts.

question_text: What does networks section do in docker-compose.yml?
explanation: networks: allows defining custom bridge networks.

question_text: Which command shows running compose services?
explanation: docker-compose ps shows status of services.

question_text: What command lists all running Docker containers?
explanation: docker ps shows only running containers by default.

question_text: Which flag makes docker run start in the background?
explanation: -d or --detach runs the container detached (in background).

question_text: What is the difference between docker stop and docker kill?
explanation: docker stop sends SIGTERM first ? allows cleanup; kill sends SIGKILL immediately.

question_text: Which command removes one or more stopped containers?
explanation: docker rm <container-id> removes stopped containers.

question_text: What does docker start <container> do?
explanation: docker start restarts a previously created but stopped container.

question_text: Which command shows the logs of a container?
explanation: docker logs <container> displays stdout/stderr of the container.

question_text: What flag follows container logs in real time?
explanation: docker logs -f <container> is like tail -f.

question_text: Which command runs a command inside a running container?
explanation: docker exec -it <container> sh opens a shell inside it.

question_text: What does the -it flag do in docker run or exec?
explanation: -it combines --interactive and --tty (keep STDIN open + pseudo-TTY).

question_text: Which command lists all Docker images locally?
explanation: All variations list local images.

question_text: What command downloads an image from a registry?

explanation: docker rmi -f <image> removes even if referenced.

question_text: What is the primary purpose of a Dockerfile?
explanation: Dockerfile defines steps to create a reproducible image.

question_text: Which instruction must be the first in most Dockerfiles?
explanation: FROM specifies the base image (almost always first non-comment line).

question_text: What does the RUN instruction do during image build?
explanation: RUN executes shell commands while building the image layer.

question_text: Which instruction is preferred for copying local files?
explanation: COPY is simple and preferred; ADD can do more (URL

question_text: What does WORKDIR instruction set?
explanation: WORKDIR /usr/src/app changes pwd for subsequent instructions.

explanation: EXPOSE 8080 informs users/tools which ports are intended.

explanation: VOLUME ["/data"] creates a mount point (anonymous volume if not bound).

question_text: Which instruction sets key-value environment variables?
explanation: ENV MY_VAR=value makes it available in build and runtime.

question_text: What is the main benefit of multi-stage builds?
explanation: Multi-stage copies only needed artifacts ? reduces image size.

question_text: Which command builds a Docker image?
explanation: All variations are valid (docker build is most common).

question_text: What flag tags the image during build?
explanation: docker build -t myapp:dev .

question_text: Which file tells Docker which files/folders to ignore in build context?
explanation: .dockerignore prevents sending unnecessary files to daemon.

question_text: What command uploads a tagged image to a registry?
explanation: docker push myrepo/app:1.0 pushes to Docker Hub or private registry.

question_text: Which command authenticates to Docker Hub or private registry?
explanation: docker login stores credentials for push/pull.

question_text: What is the default container registry when no prefix is given?
explanation: nginx ? pulls from hub.docker.com/library/nginx.

question_text: What is the standard filename for Docker Compose configuration?

topic: docker-compose down cleans up resources created by up.

question_text: Which compose section defines persistent data storage?

question_text: What is the purpose of docker pause?
explanation: docker pause suspends all processes (freeze with SIGSTOP).

explanation: docker unpause resumes processes.

question_text: What does docker top <container> show?
explanation: docker top shows ps aux style output inside container.

question_text: Which command shows detailed metadata of container/image?

question_text: What does docker stats show?

question_text: What command cleans up unused resources?

question_text: Which flag includes volumes in prune command?

question_text: What is the default storage driver on most Linux systems?
explanation: overlay2 is the current default on modern Linux.

question_text: Which command creates a named volume?

question_text: What command lists all volumes?

question_text: Which flag mounts host directory as volume?

question_text: What is the difference between -v and --mount?
explanation: --mount allows more options (type

question_text: What command inspects a specific volume?
explanation: docker volume inspect myvol shows JSON metadata.

question_text: Which network driver is default for docker run?

question_text: What does --network host do?

explanation: EXPOSE is metadata; -p actually binds host port.

question_text: Which command shows detailed network information?

explanation: --restart=always auto-restarts unless manually stopped.

question_text: Which restart policy restarts only if exit code ?0?
explanation: --restart=on-failure restarts on non-zero exit.

question_text: What command shows Docker version information?
explanation: All three show client/server versions.

question_text: Which command shows Docker daemon information?

question_text: Which command runs a one-off command in a new container?
explanation: docker run --rm alpine echo hello runs and removes.

question_text: Which flag sets environment variable at runtime?

question_text: What is the purpose of --name flag?

question_text: Which command shows real-time resource usage?

question_text: What does docker system prune -a remove?
option_c: All unused resources including unused images
explanation: -a includes all unused images (not just dangling).

question_text: What does ONBUILD instruction do?
option_a: Runs when child image is built
explanation: ONBUILD adds triggers for images based on this one.

question_text: Which command lists all Docker volumes?

question_text: What command inspects detailed volume info?

question_text: Which command removes specific volumes?

question_text: What is a bind mount?
explanation: -v /host/path:/container/path is bind mount.

question_text: Which is better for development hot-reloading?
explanation: Bind mount syncs host changes instantly (good for code editing).

question_text: What command attaches to a running container's console?

question_text: Which command creates a new container but doesn't start it?

question_text: What does --user flag do in docker run?

question_text: Which command shows Docker client and server versions?
explanation: All show version info (client + server).

question_text: Which command shows detailed system-wide information?

question_text: What is Docker Compose mainly used for?
explanation: Docker Compose defines & runs multi-container applications.

question_text: Which version of Compose file is most commonly used today?
explanation: v3.x (especially 3.8+) is standard for docker-compose and Docker Swarm.

question_text: What does depends_on in compose file do?
option_b: Waits for service to be healthy
explanation: depends_on controls startup order (not health).

question_text: Which key overrides CMD from Dockerfile in compose?

question_text: What is the purpose of restart: always in compose?
explanation: restart: always restarts unless explicitly stopped.
